creator: kingedgar@gmail.com
text:
On Wed, Mar 17, 2010 at 21:07, Pyun YongHyeon <pyunyh@gmail.com> wrote:
> On Wed, Mar 17, 2010 at 08:19:21PM -0500, Jason Garrett wrote:
>> I am not sure of anything beyond 8.0-RELEASE as I had to patch
>> immediately patch with the files in the link to get any other branch of
>> FreeBSD. I do not have a spare ethernet card, but I could throw in
>> another hard drive to test a snapshot later than what is in 8.0-RELEASE.
>>
>
> Hmm, this makes me confusing.
> Didn't you mention you used 9-CURRENT(in environment in the PR)? And
> you also said you tested the patch both on 8.0-RELEASE AND 9.0-CURRENT.
>
> FreeBSD host.name.omitted 9.0-CURRENT FreeBSD 9.0-CURRENT #0 Tue Mar 16 22:46:09 UTC 2010 root@host.name.omitted:/usr/obj/usr/src/sys/XEON amd64
>
> Anyway, if you still have problems on stable/8 or stable/7 please
> let me know. I also have BCM5761 controller and it works as
> expected.
>

I apologize for the confusion, I went straight from 8.0-RELEASE (which
does not include this chip) to csup'ing 9.0-CURRENT then build world,
buildkernel/installkernel, install world. During the csup process this
file did not change as I expect it would have so that is how I came to
figure this had not made it into the tree. Do I make any more sense?
Regardless I will be testing with a snapshot on another hard drive,
probably tomorrow. I will report back as soon as I can.
